observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Human Resource
Management unit and the Municipal Accountant to determine the
necessity, assess the duties and functions, taking into account their
expertise, experience to handle the position to come up with a
recommendation as to the ideal number of personnel to be hired for each
department.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the HRMO to
secure from the DOH the ideal number of BHW to be hired in each
barangay as provided in Section 5 of RA 7883.

We further recommended that the rates for Incentive Allowances shall
be reviewed and approved by the Local Health Board as required in
Section 7 of RA 7883.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local
Finance Committee in coordination with the MDRRMO for clearer
documentation by improving the financial reporting showing the plan
and disbursements to its proper classification, addressing the four
thematic areas, emphasizing the importance of using a significant
portion of the LDRRMF for pre-disaster preparedness and mitigation
measures.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor and the members of the
Local School Board the following courses of action:

a) Ensure that the date of receipt of a copy of the SEF Budget to the
Local Budget Officer, Local Accountant, and Local Treasurer be
appropriately logged and furnished to the said offices within seven
(7) days after the approval of the SEF Budget;

b) Submit promptly the quarterly and annual reports to the DepEd
CO, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and regional
offices of the DBM and DILG within the timeline; and

c) Monitor the posting of utilization report of SEF budget, pursuant to
the Full Disclosure Policy of the DILG.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive observe the prescribed
timeline in granting the Clothing Allowance to each eligible employee
pursuant to the above circular and that the prescribed amount be given
to the employees, except when it exceeds the PS limitation set forth in RA
7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following courses of
action:

a) Stop the granting of subsistence allowance or any similar incentives
to government personnel who receive the same type of compensations
from their respective agencies; and

b) Ensure that release of allowances, incentives and the like are
specifically authorized by law, rules and regulations so as to avoid and
prevent receipt of additional, double, or indirect compensation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and Human Resources and Management Officer to strictly
observe the mandatory monthly minimum take home pay threshold by
refraining from accommodating salary deductions for private lending
institutions and ensure compliance with the law.

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and verify the cash collections; b) Require the RCCs to prepare the covering RCD for every remittance; and c) Require the Municipal Accounting Office to review each official receipts submitted to detect errors in the computation of the amount and effect the corrections, if any.
observations.managementAction: Collections are recorded as they occur. RCD is prepared by the RCC. The Office of the Municipal Accountant is reviewing the RCD submitted from the Treasurer’s Office and returns the said report in case of discrepancy.

observations.recommendation: a) Practice sending written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale; b) Practice sending written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale; and c) Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the necessary adjustment/s in the books of accounts, if any.
observations.managementAction: Sending of written notices is practiced by the Municipal Treasurer's Office. Replacement check may be issued upon submission of the certified copy of the previously issued disbursement vouchers. Monthly verification of dates of check during bank reconciliation. Some of the said amounts have been claimed already.

observations.recommendation: a) Request from the SB members to ratify a well-defined policy guideline on the grant of assistance to come up with an ordinance, in consonance with the guidelines issued by the DSWD; b) Municipal Accountant to ascertain the completeness of the required supporting documents based on the guidelines; and c) Direct the MSWDO to prepare and submit MATRIX for the AICS program based on DSWD guidelines as an aid to the SB in the promulgation of well-defined and specific guidelines that would be consistently applied in the grant of financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: A draft ordinance was crafted and still subject for review by the Local Chief Executive. Supporting documents is already required by the accounting office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management procure telephone/communications services for cellular/mobile phones in accordance with the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184) and its IRR. Likewise, officials and employees who are entitled to the Telephone Expenses allocation/budget should be in line with item No. 5, Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-003. Further, the monthly allocation for Telephone Expenses should be at a rate not exceeding what is usual or proper, not unreasonably high and beyond just measure or amount and not in excess of reasonable limits, taking into account the prevailing rates or available promos offered by various telecommunication service providers.
observations.managementAction: Telephone expenses who are entitled to the Telephone Expenses allocation/budget are in line with RA 9184 and its IRR. Further, the monthly allocation for Telephone Expenses is being observed and regulated.
observations.reason: There are persons not entitled to telephone expenses who still claimed the communication expenses.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of using the petty cash fund for payment of regular operating expenses. Ensure that the guidelines on petty cash are strictly observed and that procurements be included in the Annual Procurement Plan and be procured in bulk from DBM Procurement Services or in accordance with RA 9184. The PCV as required shall be practiced and the required accounting reports embodied in Section 33 of GAM Volume I for LGUs be complied with.
observations.managementAction: The guidelines on the use of petty cash are strictly observed. The practice has been discontinued and procurements are now properly included in the Annual Procurement Plan.

observations.recommendation: a) Enjoin the General Services Officer (GSO) and the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) for the identified unserviceable properties and initiate the process of disposal of the same; and b) Direct the GSO to submit the pertinent documents to the Accounting Office as basis to derecognize the mentioned unserviceable properties from the books of accounts, pursuant to existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and the Appraisal and Disposal Committee prepared the IIRUP and initiated disposal procedures. The pertinent documents were submitted to the Accounting Office for derecognition.
observations.reason: Disposable of unserviceable properties has not yet commenced.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Mayor direct the officials concerned to strictly observe the prescribed period in the submission of the financial report of collections and deposits to COA. Likewise, ensure that all executive officials and employees of the municipality faithfully discharge their duties and functions as provided for by regulations.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement vouchers and supporting documents were timely submitted thereafter. The municipality is enforcing compliance with submission timelines.

observations.recommendation: a) Strictly require the barangay Solid Waste Management (SWM) Committees that their MRFs should comply with the minimum requirements for segregation at source and storage of solid waste pending collection, as set forth under the IRR of RA 9003; and b) Enjoin all barangays SWM Committees: (i) to establish their MRF in a barangay-owned or leased land or any suitable open space to be determined by the barangay through its Sanggunian; and, (ii) design the MRFs in such a way that it can receive, sort, process and store compostable, residual and recyclable material efficiently and in an environmentally sound manner.
observations.managementAction: The barangay SWM Committees were directed to establish and design their MRFs in compliance with RA 9003. Provision of funds for MRF establishment and capacity building was initiated.

observations.recommendation: Assess its existing organizational structure so that the ideal number of personnel to be established, thereby, avoiding hiring an excessive number of job order personnel. Hiring job order personnel should be undertaken based on the necessity to avoid waste of funds, as prescribed in Sections 76 and 77 of R.A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: LGU to assess its organizational structure and prepare staffing pattern; HR to prepare guidelines on hiring JOs.
observations.reason: A similar observation is incorporated in this report (Finding No. 10).

observations.recommendation: a) Create a committee that will be responsible for the preparation of a Feasibility Study and a 5-year Business Plan for the existing LEE. Henceforth, future LEEs to be established by the LGU, should be supported with a Feasibility Study and a 5-year Business Plan; and b) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal treasurer to prepare and maintain separate books of accounts and financial reports to facilitate evaluation on the operations of the existing LEEs.
observations.managementAction: Committee created to prepare Feasibility Study and Business Plan. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare separate books when LEEs become operational.
observations.reason: No Feasibility Study submitted and the implementation of the LEE was deferred; no separate books of accounts since the LEE operation was not materialized.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Engineer to issue the Final Certificate of Acceptance after the lapse of the defect liability period or one (1) year after project completion, and after ensuring that repair works on damages, if any, were satisfactorily taken by the contractor at its own expense.
observations.managementAction: Final Certificates of Acceptance will be issued only after lapse of defect liability period and after ensuring repairs are satisfactorily done by contractor.

observations.recommendation: a) The MPDC to identify the appropriate PPAs which should be prioritized, determine if the project is to be implemented by phases or not, and coordinate with the Local Finance Committee in order to secure sufficient funding for its PPA identified and prioritized; b) The Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit the Program of Work (POW), plans/drawings and technical specification with detailed breakdown of the cost up to the completion of the project, separate from the POW per phases, so as to appropriately allocate funds and to ensure continuity of the works.
observations.managementAction: MPDC to identify and prioritize PPAs; Municipal Engineer to prepare detailed POW, plans and technical specifications per phase.

observations.recommendation: a) Take action on the process and procedures on the write-off of the related receivable accounts; and b) Provide Allowance for Impairment corresponding to the uncollectible accounts pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to reduce the affected receivable accounts to its appropriate carrying amounts.
observations.managementAction: Request for write-off already submitted to COA and reported on a quarterly basis. Efforts exerted to locate accountable officials and ascertain whereabouts of debtors.
observations.reason: Some of the accountable officials are no longer connected to the LGU and their locations are not yet identified; some are deceased.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Persons Responsible to settle the audit suspension, otherwise it will mature to an audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: There is an ongoing effort to identify details of the suspension. Persons responsible have been requested to settle; some amounts could not be settled due to lack of details and occurrence during prior administrations.
observations.reason: A portion of the amount could not be settled due to no details and that it transpired during the term of their predecessors.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all parcels of land owned by the Municipality be registered under the name of the Municipality for its protection from third party claims.
observations.managementAction: Land titling is in process; application and processing of titles ongoing.
observations.reason: Land titling is still in process.

observations.recommendation: Submit the lacking documentation for the disbursement amounting to ₱365,000.00; submit the Disbursement Voucher for the purchase of NFA Rice amounting to ₱187,500.00; and submit the Liquidation Report for the cash advance amounting to ₱100,000.00 taken from the 30% quick response fund.
observations.managementAction: Fund Utilization Report for LDRRMF were submitted on a monthly basis and the DVs and liquidation reports for the subject transactions were already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: Require the Former Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately all undeposited collections pursuant to Section 69 of PD 1445 and section 32 of the NGAS Manual to protect government funds from the risks of loss.
observations.managementAction: Written demand letters were sent to the former Municipal Treasurer; parts of the undeposited collections have been reclassified and some amounts returned to sender.
observations.reason: Response received but some amounts remain outstanding; letters mailed via post office were returned to sender for the reason that the person went abroad.

observations.recommendation:
1.8. We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following courses of
action:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor and verify the official
receipts submitted by the RCCs to accurately report the collections
as they occur;
b) Require the RCCs to prepare the covering RCD for every
remittance; and
c) Require the Municipal Accounting Office to review each official
receipts submitted to detect errors in the computation of the amount
and effect the corrections, if any.

2.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to:
a) Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale
checks. A replacement check may be issued upon submission of the
stale check; a certified copy of the previously issued disbursement
vouchers;
b) Practice sending written notice to the payee at least one month before
a check becomes stale; and
c) Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the necessary
adjustment/s in the books of accounts, if any.

3.16. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive the following courses
of action:
a) Request from the SB members to ratify a well-defined policy
guideline on the grant of assistance to come up with an ordinance, in
consonance with the guidelines issued by the DSWD;
b) Municipal Accountant to ascertain the completeness of the required
supporting documents based on the guidelines; and
c) Direct the MSWDO to prepare and submit MATRIX for the AICS
program based on DSWD guidelines as an aid to the SB in the
promulgation of well-defined and specific guidelines that would be
consistently applied in the grant of financial assistance. Likewise,
requires her to maintain objectivity, in determining the type, kind,
and amount of financial assistance to be recommended for approval
of the appropriate authority.

4.17. We recommended that the management procure
telephone/communications services for cellular/mobile phones in
accordance with the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184)
and its IRR. Likewise, officials and employees who are entitled to the
Telephone Expenses allocation/budget should be in line with item No. 5,
Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-003.
The Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to
require that all claims for telephone/communication services under
MOOE be supported with a Statement of Account/Bill from
telecommunication service providers, official receipts/invoices and
certification that calls are official as required in COA Circular No. 2012-
001. Likewise, official receipts/invoices printed in thermal papers should
be photocopied.
Further, the monthly allocation for Telephone Expenses should be at a
rate not exceeding what is usual or proper, not unreasonably high and
beyond just measure or amount and not in excess of reasonable limits,
taking into account the prevailing rates or available promos offered by
various telecommunication service providers.
Moreover, we recommend that the municipality submit a copy of existing
memorandum or order and Sanggunian Bayan Resolution detailing the
guidelines for all claims of the Telephone Expense and the corresponding
rates entitled for each official or employee.

5.7. We recommended that Management strictly observe pertinent sections
of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, and the Documentary
requirements outlined in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, in
the grant, utilization, and replenishment of the Petty Cash Fund.
Henceforth, stop the practice of using the petty cash fund for payment of
regular operating expenses.
We further recommended discontinuing the practice of procuring office
supplies, materials, and tarpaulin printing for different activities
through reimbursement out of PCF. Instead, all procurements should be
included in the Annual Procurement Plan and be procured in bulk from
DBM Procurement Services for the commonly used supplies and in
accordance with the provision on RA 9184 for other supplies and
materials.
Moreover, the use of the PCV as required shall be practiced and the
required accounting reports embodied in Section 33 of GAM Volume I
for LGUs be complied with.

6.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive take the following
courses of action:
a) Enjoin the General Services Officer (GSO) and the Appraisal and
Disposal Committee to prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report
of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) for the identified unserviceable
properties and initiate the process of disposal of the same; and
b) Direct the GSO to submit the pertinent documents to the Accounting
Office as basis to derecognize the mentioned unserviceable
properties from the books of accounts, pursuant to existing
accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

7.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the officials
concerned to strictly observe the prescribed period in the submission of
the financial transaction documents to COA. Likewise, ensure that all
executive officials and employees of the municipality faithfully discharge
their duties and functions as provided for by regulations.

9.10. We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the MENRO
the following courses of action:
a) Strictly require the barangay Solid Waste Management (SWM)
Committees that their MRFs should comply with the minimum
requirements for segregation at source and storage of solid waste
pending collection, as set forth under the IRR of RA 9003; and
b) Enjoin all barangays SWM Committees: (i) to establish their MRF
in a barangay-owned or leased land or any suitable open space to
be determined by the barangay through its Sanggunian; and, (ii)
design the MRFs in such a way that it can receive, sort, process and
store compostable, residual and recyclable material efficiently and
in an environmentally sound manner.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the treasury and
accounting records to bring into agreement both records pursuant to Sec.
58 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002 to assure reliability of the
reported account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct:
a. The General Services Officer and the Livestock Officer
Designate to conduct an inventory of the Biological Assets, and
to prepare an inventory report thereon showing the fair market
value of each specie and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant for proper recording and recognition of the
Biological Assets in the books; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for
biological assets, update the same based on the report of the
General Services Officer or the Livestock Officer Designate and
ensure that proper accounting and recognition is made in the
Financial Statements to bring into agreement the two account
balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to provide an allowance for Impairment-Other Receivables
while waiting for the result of the request for write off of said accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to:
a) Require the Revenue Collecting Clerks to remit the collection
had by them on a daily basis and to prepare and submit the
corresponding report of collections and deposits thereon;
b) Conduct periodic inventory of accountable forms reported on
hand and issued by the RCC in the Report of Accountability for
Accountable Forms at every remittance made before accepting
and affixing her signature on the RCD;
c) Reconcile the status of AFs reported in the monthly RAAF with
the actual balance on hand, and effect any
corrections/adjustments, to have an accurate report of the
accountable forms on hand and issued for the period covered by
the report;
d) Limit to one booklet of accountable forms issued to RCC at a
time and ensure that a new one shall only be released when the
booklet was already fully issued and remitted; and
e) Observe the prescribed period in the remittance of the
collections as provided in Sec 29 of the NGAS Manual in order
to avoid accumulation of cash collections on the hands of the
AOs.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to
include all benefits and remuneration received by the Municipal officials
and employees in the computation of their respective taxable income in
accordance with RMC No. 23-2012 and section 6 (B) of Revenue
Regulations No. 11-2018, and withhold the correct amount of taxes and
remit the same on time to avoid the possibility of incurring penalties in
the future.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards to monitor issuances of the
inventories.

Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the General
Services Officer to:
a) Maintain stock cards, and direct the Supply Officer to submit
the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), together
with the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and other pertinent
supporting documents, to the Accounting Office for recognition
of expense of issued/distributed inventories; and
b) Conduct periodic physical count of the inventory items, prepare
and submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant, for
the latter to come up with an accurate recording and
determination of the inventory account balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Engineer to the following courses of action:
a) Require the contractor to post signboard, tarpaulins or similar
materials as soon as the award has been made that must be
suitably framed for outdoor display at the project location; and
b) Ensure that the cost of signboards was included in the project
cost, provided such conform to the specifications set forth under
COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Engineer to issue the Final Certificate of Acceptance after the lapse of
the defect liability period or one (1) year after project completion, and
after ensuring that repair works on damages, if any, were satisfactorily
taken by the contractor at its own expense.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
a) The MPDC to identify the appropriate PPAs which should be
prioritized, determine if the project is to be implemented by
phases or not, and coordinate with the Local Finance Committee
in order to secure sufficient funding for its PPA identified and
prioritized; and
b) The Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit the Program of
Work (POW), plans/drawings and technical specification with
detailed breakdown of the cost up to the completion of the
project, separate from the POW per phases, so as to
appropriately allocate funds and to ensure continuity of the
works, and to ensure that the Programs of Work shall cover at
least a usable portion of the project.

We recommended for the LCE to take the following courses of action:
a. Require the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that the
contract awardees for the procurement of goods understand and
comply with the terms and conditions provided in the contract.
Moreover, consider in the post-qualification of bids and
evaluation of received quotations, for purposes of awarding the
contracts, the performance and compliance to contract terms of
bidder-supplier in past transactions. Further, make sure that the
PO should be properly accomplished to show relevant
information; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that penalties as a
result of delays in the delivery of goods by suppliers are
computed and deduct any money due or which may become due
to them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should ensure that the
development projects be optimally utilized for the attainment of
desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer and all other offices
concerned should implement the PPAs within the schedule of
implementation as indicated in the Annual Investment Plan and without
further delay to achieve optimal benefits from the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board:
a) Review and approve the SEF Annual Budget for the ensuing
year not later than October 31 of the current year; and
b) Implement the planned and approved PPAs during the year and
utilize the funds fully in order to achieve the purpose for which
the fund was established.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Department
Heads to:
a) Strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on prioritization, and
training of personnel responsible for planning and
implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities to
ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related
undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of
the services, protection, and other assistance that may be
provided; and
b) Observe the scheduled GAD Planning and Budgeting as
provided by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated
January 12, 2016 to facilitate timely submission to the Provincial
Planning and Development Office and the subsequent review
and endorsement by the DILG Provincial Office.

1.7	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the accounting policy on non-inclusion of residual value in the computation of the depreciation expense for the depreciable component of the road network, prepare the adjusting entry to correct the error committed, maintain subsidiary records for road and road components and disclose the details, component and the breakdown of cost in the Notes to the Financial Statements as embodied in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

2.10	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the General Services Officer (GSO) and the Disposal Committee to prepare the corresponding Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the identified unserviceable properties and initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and the rules provided in COA Circular No. 92-386; and
After the disposal, require the GSO to submit the necessary documents, together with the official receipt to the Accounting Office, as the basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

3.6	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities.  This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors to post the required warranty securities.
3.7	Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR in the implementation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final acceptance.

4.9	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to submit monthly the Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project/Activity to the audit team.  We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger for the BGCM account for easy monitoring/tracking of its utilization.

5.7	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
MSWDO to cause the immediate submission of the required documents as prescribed under the rules and procedures of DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 04 to ensure that the beneficiary of the social amelioration provisions comply with the documentary requirements and guarantee attainment of the primary purpose of the social protection program; and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that the liquidation documents are complete and sufficient.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.

6.10	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC and Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documentary requirements as provided in Annex A of this AOM, in compliance to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020, respectively.

7.7	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MSWDO to provide the accounting unit copies of the RIS/distribution list of relief goods. Further, require the GSO to ensure the submission of the Monthly Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Utilization of Relief Goods.

8.12	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require  the concerned officials to prepare the (1) Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-Kind Donations; (2) Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; (3) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods; and (4) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated PPE; and furnish the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team within ten working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular 2014-002 and 2020-009.

9.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to submit the lacking documents enumerated above. Henceforth, ensure that procurements are properly supported with relevant documents before any payments are made and strictly observe the prescribed period of submission of perfected contracts/purchase orders as provided for under COA Circular 2009-001.

10.6	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers with the relevant supporting documents. Likewise, strictly observed the deadline on the submission of accounts and other reports as required under Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006, dated September 15, 2009.

11.5	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Supply Officer to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its government vehicles subjected to repairs.
Require that the Supply Officer prepare the Certificate of Acceptance and Report of Waste Material and approved by the LCE.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a ledger card for the expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every vehicle owned by the LGU to monitor whether the property is economically repairable.

12.5	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accountable Officers to strictly comply with the preparation and submission of the monthly RAAF as basis for monitoring on the movement of accountable forms at their possession and to ensure reliability of the reported monthly balances of inventory.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all accountable officials and employees of the LGU with outstanding cash advances to immediately settle their cash advances by submitting the necessary documents as evidence of the disbursement made or thru cash refund of the unused cash advances.  Likewise, exert extra effort to locate the whereabouts of those who have retired or out of service, and cause the liquidation of their outstanding cash advance.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were already sent to those officers and employees with outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Balances of Unliquidated advances by the SDO still remained unsettled as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

1)  Take action on the process and procedures on the write-off of the related receivable accounts; and

2)	Provide Allowance for Impairment corresponding to the uncollectible accounts pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to reduce the affected receivable accounts to its appropriate carrying amounts.
observations.managementAction: The management is still finalizing the records needed for the request for write-off.
observations.reason: The LGU made a request for write-off.  However, said request was not supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate our audit recommendation to:

a. Conduct the physical inventory of all PPEs. Prescribed composition of the committee for the conduct of physical inventory is provided in Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.  While prescribed forms to be used in the reporting is provided in the Manual for NGAS.

b. Reconcile the balances recorded in the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) of the Accounting Office with the balances reported in the Property Cards (PC) maintained by the General Service Office. 

c. Require all heads of the department to prepare a list of all existing properties under their respective offices and submit the same to the inventory committee to facilitate the inventory taking.
observations.managementAction: The management has created the inventory team and conducted physical inventory on LGUs PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

The Municipal Engineering Office:

•	Provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road component for road projects; as well as the report on the repair/maintenance of each local road network;

The General Services Officer/Inventory Committee:

•	Conduct a Physical Count of the Road Network (RPCLRN) and submit the corresponding report thereon.

•	Maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each roads and local road network;

The Municipal Accountant:

•	Reconcile the report from the Office of the Municipal Engineer and the accounting records and prepare the necessary adjusting entry for the discrepancies. Prepare the adjusting entry to correct the error committed,

•	Maintain subsidiary records for road and road components.

•	Prepare lapsing schedule for the depreciation of the depreciable components of the Local Road Network.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has created the inventory team but still on the process of reconciliation of records.

cash refund of the unused cash advances. Likewise, exert extra effort to

cause the liquidation of their outstanding cash advance.

related receivable accounts; and

uncollectible accounts pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to
reduce the affected receivable accounts to its appropriate carrying
amounts.

a. Conduct the physical inventory of all PPEs. Prescribed composition
of the committee for the conduct of physical inventory is provided
in Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

the Manual for NGAS.

Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) of the Accounting Office with
the balances reported in the Property Cards (PC) maintained by

inventory committee to facilitate the inventory taking.

submit the corresponding report thereon.
Maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each roads
and local road network;
The Municipal Accountant:
Reconcile the report from the Office of the Municipal Engineer
and the accounting records and prepare the necessary adjusting
entry for the discrepancies. Prepare the adjusting entry to correct
the error committed,
Maintain subsidiary records for road and road components.
Prepare lapsing schedule for the depreciation of the depreciable
components of the Local Road Network.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry for the stale checks of
₱228,573.39 so that the Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts are fairly
presented in the financial statements, and to submit regularly and promptly
the required paid checks and debit/credit memos together with the bank
reconciliation statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require all accountable
officials and employees of the LGU with outstanding cash advances to
immediately settle their cash advances by submitting the necessary documents
as proof of the disbursement made or thru cash refund of the unused cash
advances. If request for liquidation remained not acted, we recommend for
the issuance of demand letter. If it is still not acted by the accountable officer,
appropriate legal action shall be filed against the employees. For employees
who are connected to other government agency, seek assistance from the
respective agency head by sending the demand letter thru them. Also, exert
extra effort to locate the whereabouts of those who have retired or out of
service, and cause the liquidation of their outstanding cash advance.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the
provisions of Section 20 of the New Government Accounting System Manual
for LGUs. A duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the
corresponding amount due and collectible for the year be prepared and
submitted to the Municipal Accountant which should serve as the basis for
recording the Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable
at the beginning of the year.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
(a) Record regular purchases of inventories using the perpetual
inventory method
(b) Conduct regular count of inventories on hand;
(c) Prepare the required inventory reports, schedules, supplies ledger
cards and stock cards;
(d) Use/accomplish the prescribed inventory forms; and
(e) Ensure coordination between the end-users and the accounting office
for the submission of required reports and the proper accounting
for the inventories.

We recommend that all parcels of land owned by the Municipality be
registered under the name of the Municipality for its protection from third party
claims.

We reiterate our recommendation that management strictly comply
with the required physical count of all properties at least once a year and to
submit a copy of the Inventory Report to the audit Team not later than
January 31 and every year thereafter to ensure the accuracy and existence of
the PPE accounts presented in the financial statements

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer
maintain an updated Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively,
and shall henceforth conduct periodic reconciliation of the balances of both
records.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive fast track the
implementation of the projects so that constituents could benefit the intended
purpose of the funds received. Likewise, submit the lacking documents and
the liquidation reports on the completed projects in conformance to Section 4.9
& Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 19, 1994.

We recommend that Local School Board strictly adhere to the provisions of
DepEd, DBM, DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and the National Budget No. 567 dated
January 3, 2017.

We recommend that management submit the following:
a. Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the
15th day after the end of each month to the COA auditor of the
LGU;
b. Lacking documentation for the disbursement amounting to
₱365,000.00;
c. Disbursement Voucher for the purchase of NFA Rice amounting to
₱187,500.00; and
d. Liquidation Report for the cash advance amounting to ₱100,000.00
taken from the 30% quick response fund.

Furthermore, we recommend that management observe the accounting
and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 by
following the prescribed format in disclosing the amount allocated for MOOE
and Capital Outlay.

We recommend the Solid Waste Management Board to keep updating
the status of the amended 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan compliant
with the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003, and implement the
plan to ensure efficient disposal of Municipality’s solid wastes.

We recommend that the MCPC shall propose and implement
programs, projects and activities as envisioned in DILG Memorandum
Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 and submit the lacking distribution
list/list of recipients with corresponding signatures.

We recommend that Management fully implement the targeted
programs, projects and activities under 20% Development Fund as presented
in the AIP in accordance with the provisions of Section 476 of RA 7160 and
DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017
for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of
Joint Memorandum Circular No.2013-01 of the PWC-DILG-NEDA-DBM or
Guidelines in the Localization of Magna Carta for Women.

We recommend that management execute Job Order/Contract of
Service individually and provide the specific job descriptions or details on the
expected outputs in order to determine the necessity of hiring Job Order
personnel. Furthermore, we recommend that the Job Order worker hired affix
signature to acknowledge the contract.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or the Designated Disbursing
Officer exercise due care in the release of payments for every transaction and to
stamp “PAID” on the payrolls, vouchers and its supporting documents upon
release of payment to comply with established rules and regulations.
